case_title: JOHN MOORE VS. C & S WHOLESALE GROCERS INC. et al

docket_entries.description: NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF TRIAL, JURY TRIAL SET FOR SEP-24-2012 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON MAR-02-2012 IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-26-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
DISMISSAL WITHOUT PREJUDICE OF COMPLAINT AS TO DEFENDANT DOES 1 TO 150, INCL.
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED APPROVING PROP 65 SETTLEMENT AND CONSENT JUDGMENT AND JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO TERMS OF PROP 65 SETTLEMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF MOORE, JOHN
AFFIDAVIT IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FILED BY PLAINTIFF MOORE, JOHN

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JOHN MOORE'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROP 65 SETTLEMENT AND CONSENT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED AND THE SETTLEMENT COMPLIES WITH HEALTH AND SAFETY CODE SECTION 25249.7. WHILE THE COURT HAS SOME CONCERNS ABOUT THE REASONABLENESS OF THE FEES AGREED TO BY THE PARTIES AND THE SUFFICIENCY OF THE EVIDENTIARY SHOWING MADE BY PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL OF THE FEES, UNLESS AND UNTIL THE COURT HAS DEVELOPED A BETTER SYSTEM OF EVALUATING THE REASONABLENESS OF FEES AGREED TO BY THE PARTIES IN PROPOSITION 65 CASES WHERE THERE IS NO OPPOSITION MADE TO THOSE FEES, THE COURT IS SATISFIED THAT THE SHOWING MADE HERE IS ADEQUATE, ALTHOUGH PERHAPS BARELY SO. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.
NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-07-2012 Vacated

Notice Of Motion To Approve Prop 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-26-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Josh Vorghees Esq., of The Chanler Group, appearing for plaintiff and moving party.
Daniel Fox, Esq., of K & L Gates, Tel. 415.882.8017, appearing for defendant via court call.

Neither party opposed the tentative ruling. Therefore, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JOHN MOORE'S Motion To Approve Prop 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment is Granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with Health and Safety Code section 25249.7. While the court has some concerns about the reasonableness of the fees agreed to by the parties and the sufficiency of the evidentiary showing made by plaintiff for approval of the fees, unless and until the court has developed a better system of evaluating the reasonableness of fees agreed to by the parties in Proposition 65 cases where there is no opposition made to those fees, the court is satisfied that the showing made here is adequate, although perhaps barely so. Order and Judgment signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-21-2012 Set For Trial On Sep-24-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
